query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Conformist', 'A person who conforms to the standards of a group', 'The author hoped the new policy would conformist tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conformity', 'Compliance with standards or social norms', '“His conformity to tradition was evident in his behavior.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Confound', 'To cause surprise or confusion in someone, especially by not acting according to their expectations', 'Over time, the community learned to confound its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Confront', 'To stand or meet facing, to oppose, to challenge', 'Critics argued that the plan might confront the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congeal', 'To change from a liquid to solid state perhaps by cold', 'Critics argued that the plan might congeal the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congenial', 'Having the same or very similar nature or personality', 'What seemed congenial at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congenital', 'Present since birth', 'The essay highlights the congenital of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congest', 'To overfill or overcrowd', 'Over time, the community learned to congest its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congregate', 'To collect into an assembly, to gather together', 'The author hoped the new policy would congregate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congregation', 'A gathering of faithful in a place of worship', 'Scholars noted a striking congregation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congruence', 'Agreement or harmony; compatibility', 'Her congruence became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congruent', 'Agreeing, accordant, congruous', 'What seemed congruent at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Congruity', 'The quality of agreeing, the quality of being suitable', 'Scholars noted a striking congruity between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Coniferous', 'Bearing cones, as the pine and cypress', 'The passage presents a coniferous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conjecture', 'A guess or speculation without complete evidence', '“His theory was based on conjecture rather than facts.”', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conjoin', 'To join, to combine', 'Over time, the community learned to conjoin its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conjugal', 'Of, or relating to marriage, or the relationship of spouses', 'The author hoped the new policy would conjugal tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conjugate', 'To list the inflected forms of a verb for each person', 'The author hoped the new policy would conjugate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conjugation', 'The inflection of verbs', 'The essay highlights the conjugation of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conjunction', 'The formation of a link or connection between things', 'Her conjunction became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Connive', 'To cooperate with others secretly in order to commit a crime', 'The author hoped the new policy would connive tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Connoisseur', 'An expert judge in matters of taste', 'Scholars noted a striking connoisseur between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Connotation', 'The implied or associated meaning of a word', '“The word ‘home’ has a warm connotation.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Connote', 'To signify beyond its literal or principal meaning', 'Critics argued that the plan might connote the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Connubial', 'Of or relating to the state of being married', 'Over time, the community learned to connubial its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conquer', 'To defeat in combat, to subjugate', 'Over time, the community learned to conquer its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consanguineous', 'Related by birth, having the same ancestry', 'The passage presents a consanguineous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conscience', 'The moral sense of right and wrong', 'The essay highlights the conscience of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conscientious', 'Thorough, careful, or vigilant', 'The passage presents a conscientious portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conscript', 'To enrol compulsorily, to draft', 'Over time, the community learned to conscript its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consecrate', 'To make or declare (something) sacred', 'The author hoped the new policy would consecrate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consecutive', 'Following, in succession, without interruption', 'The reviewer described the tone as consecutive, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consensus', 'General agreement among a group of people', 'Scholars noted a striking consensus between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consequenc', 'The effects or outcomes of an action or situation', 'Scholars noted a striking consequenc between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consequential', 'Significant or important', '“His actions had consequential effects on the company.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conservationist', 'People who work to protect the environment', '“Conservationists fought to save the rainforest.”', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conservatism', 'A political philosophy that advocates traditional values', 'Scholars noted a striking conservatism between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conservative', 'Tending to resist change', 'The author hoped the new policy would conservative tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conservatory', 'A college for the study of classical music or other arts', 'Her conservatory became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consign', 'To transfer to the custody of  for sale, transport, etc', 'The author hoped the new policy would consign tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consignee', 'The person to whom a shipment is to be delivered', 'The author hoped the new policy would consignee tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consignor', 'The person who sends the shipment', 'Scholars noted a striking consignor between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consistency', 'Degree of density, viscosity, firmness, etc', 'The essay highlights the consistency of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consolation', 'The act of comforting someone', 'Scholars noted a striking consolation between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Console', 'To comfort (someone) in a time of grief, disappointment, etc', 'The author hoped the new policy would console tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consolidate', 'Combined or made stronger', '“The company consolidated its branches into one headquarters.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consonance', 'Agreement or compatibility between opinion or actions', 'Scholars noted a striking consonance between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consonant', 'Characterized by harmony or agreement', 'The passage presents a consonant portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Consort', 'A wife, husband, or companion', 'Scholars noted a striking consort between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Conspicuous', 'Easily noticeable or attracting attention', '“Her bright red dress was conspicuous in the crowd.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
